name: CUA Checkers spec ledger
description: Authoritative spec list. Each item has status flag. Findings from tests and adversarial reviews append new spec entries. Iterate until all status=done.

process.tdd: Red-Green-Refactor. Write failing test first, implement minimum to pass, refactor.
process.coverage: 100% line+branch coverage required for src/ before push.
process.findings_handling: Every finding from any test/review becomes a new spec entry with status=todo. Loop continues until specs.json has zero todo|in_progress|review entries.
process.delegation: Orchestrator only. All implementation tasks delegated to sonnet-model team agents in parallel where possible. Opus (this assistant) does NOT write code or run tests directly.
process.team: Sonnet team agents created via TeamCreate, addressable via SendMessage.

specs.title: Browser-based 2D checkers game
CUA-friendly DOM: stable square IDs (sq-<file><rank>), data-square, data-piece, aria-label
JS programmatic API on window.checkers (getState, getLegalMoves, move, moveSan, reset, undo, onChange)
Permissive rules: no forced captures, multi-jump chains may halt at any reachable square
specs.notes: Implemented in pure rules engine; ported from legacy.

specs.title: Move-hint toggle: always / off / first N moves
Convert app to Next.js (App Router) with custom Node server
specs.evidence: package.json, next.config.mjs, jsconfig.json present; server.js TBD

specs.title: Server tracks all moves authoritatively (in-memory game store keyed by gameId)
REST API route for external agent: GET /api/games, GET /api/games/:id, GET /api/games/:id/moves, POST /api/games/:id/moves
WebSocket /ws endpoint for browser↔server move passing and live state sync; supports query params game and as (red|black|observer)
Visual role indicator: BACKGROUND or HEADER AREA color reflects role (red=red, black=neutral/dark, observer=blue/green). Not a banner.
specs.notes: User clarified: header area or full-page tint, not a small banner. Distinct color per role.

specs.title: Player role assignment: first joiner = red, second = black, rest = observers; freed on disconnect; client may request role via ?as=…
Move-hint toggle present in Next.js client (parity with legacy)
JS programmatic API present on window.checkers in Next.js client (parity with legacy, with WS-driven state)
CUA-friendly DOM in Next.js client (parity with legacy)
Spec file: specs.json maintained with status flags; loop until all entries reach done
Task manifest at .agents/task-manifest.md with phased plan
Vitest test runner with @vitest/coverage-v8; npm test produces coverage report
100% line and branch coverage of src/ enforced via coverage thresholds
Rules engine unit tests: initial board, simple moves, captures, multi-jump prefixes, king promotion, mid-jump promotion stops chain, game over detection
Store unit tests: role assignment ordering, requested role honoring, observer fallback, disconnect frees role, attemptMove turn enforcement, undo/reset
REST API integration tests with running Next server: GET list, GET state, GET moves, POST moves (success + role mismatch + illegal)
WebSocket integration test: two clients connect, role assignment, move broadcast, presence updates
Client component tests (React testing-library + jsdom): renders board, role color theming, click handlers gated by turn/role, hint toggle behavior
Per-commit adversarial review via codex-rescue with gpt-5.4-mini xhigh reasoning; findings logged into specs.json
Pre-push adversarial review via codex-rescue with gpt-5.5 high reasoning; findings logged into specs.json
Sonnet team created via TeamCreate; orchestrator delegates all implementation; no opus implementation work
